name: markdown-runner
description: Enables the seamless execution of code blocks in any language within Markdown files in VS Code

markdownRunner.compilerSettings.markdownDescription: Compiler commands are optional and run before interpreter commands. Enter the temporary file extension, then the compile command. Available placeholders: `${path}`, `${dir}`, `${name}`, `${ext}`, `${exe}`. Click ↻ to restore defaults.

markdownRunner.interpreterSettings.markdownDescription: Interpreter commands are mandatory and run after compiler commands. Enter the temporary file extension, then the interpreter command. Available placeholders: `${path}`, `${dir}`, `${name}`, `${ext}`, `${exe}`. The first tag in each language list is used for the CodeLens button. Click ↻ to restore defaults.

markdownRunner.pythonPath.markdownDescription: Enable to add the Markdown file's parent directory to Python's `sys.path`, allowing you to import modules from that directory.

markdownRunner.activateOnQuarto.markdownDescription: Enable to activate the extension on Quarto (.qmd) files.

markdownRunner.outputEncoding.markdownDescription: Encoding used to decode `Run on Markdown` process output via [iconv-lite](https://github.com/pillarjs/iconv-lite). Pick a common value from the list, or set any other [supported encoding](https://github.com/pillarjs/iconv-lite/wiki/Supported-Encodings) directly in `settings.json`. Change to `gbk` if non-ASCII characters appear corrupted on some Windows systems.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
